phrases.def: 意大利面条式结构（扁平灵活的组织结构）

sentences.en: Organisation is big business.
sentences.zh: “组织管理”是一门大生意。

grammar.type: 主系表
grammar.note: 极简单句，主干 Organisation is big business；big business 意为“大生意、大买卖”。

sentences.en: Whether it is of our lives – all those inboxes and calendars – or how companies are structured, a multi-billion dollar industry helps to meet this need.
sentences.zh: 无论是对我们生活的组织安排——所有那些收件箱和日程表——还是对公司结构的组织，一个数十亿美元的产业都在帮助满足这一需求。

grammar.type: Whether...or... 让步状语 + 破折号插入
grammar.note: Whether it is of our lives... or how companies are structured 为 whether...or... 引导的让步状语从句，两破折号间 all those inboxes and calendars 为举例插入；主句 a multi-billion dollar industry helps to meet this need。

sentences.en: We have more strategies for time management, project management and self-organisation than at any other time in human history.
sentences.zh: 如今我们拥有的时间管理、项目管理和自我组织的策略，比人类历史上任何时候都多。

grammar.type: 比较结构 than
grammar.note: 主干 We have more strategies for...；than at any other time in human history 为比较对象，more... than at any other time 表“比任何时候都多”。

sentences.en: We are told that we ought to organise our company, our home life, our week, our day and even our sleep, all as a means to becoming more productive.
sentences.zh: 人们告诉我们，应该把公司、家庭生活、一周、一天乃至睡眠都组织安排好，而这一切都是为了变得更有效率。

grammar.type: 被动语态 + 宾语从句 + as a means to
grammar.note: 主干 We are told that...（被动）；从句 we ought to organise our company... and even our sleep，多个宾语并列；all as a means to becoming more productive 为方式状语，as a means to (doing) 意为“作为……的手段”。

sentences.en: Every week, countless seminars and workshops take place around the world to tell a paying public that they ought to structure their lives in order to achieve this.
sentences.zh: 每周，世界各地都会举办无数的研讨会和工作坊，告诉付费的公众：他们应当把自己的生活安排得井井有条，以实现这一目标。

grammar.type: 不定式目的 + 宾语从句
grammar.note: 主干 countless seminars and workshops take place around the world；to tell a paying public that... 为不定式作目的状语，that they ought to structure their lives 为宾语从句；in order to achieve this 为目的状语。

sentences.en: This rhetoric has also crept into the thinking of business leaders and entrepreneurs, much to the delight of self-proclaimed perfectionists with the need to get everything right.
sentences.zh: 这套说辞也悄然渗入了商界领袖和企业家的思维之中，令那些自诩为完美主义者、事事都要做到无懈可击的人大为欣喜。

grammar.type: 现在完成时 + much to the delight of 结构
grammar.note: 主干 This rhetoric has also crept into the thinking of...，creep into 意为“悄悄进入”；much to the delight of self-proclaimed perfectionists 为“to one's delight”的强化结构，表“令……非常高兴”；with the need to get everything right 为 perfectionists 的后置定语。

sentences.en: The number of business schools and graduates has massively increased over the past 50 years, essentially teaching people how to organise well.
sentences.zh: 在过去50年里，商学院和毕业生的数量大幅增加，其本质上是在教人们如何把事情组织好。

grammar.type: 现在完成时 + 现在分词状语
grammar.note: 主干 The number of business schools and graduates has massively increased；essentially teaching people how to organise well 为现在分词短语作伴随/结果状语，how to organise well 为其宾语。

sentences.en: Ironically, however, the number of businesses that fail has also steadily increased.
sentences.zh: 然而，具有讽刺意味的是，倒闭的企业数量也在稳步上升。

grammar.type: 定语从句 + 副词状语
grammar.note: 主干 the number of businesses... has also steadily increased；that fail 为定语从句修饰 businesses；Ironically, however 为插入性评注/转折状语。

words.def: 讽刺地；具有讽刺意味的是

sentences.en: Work-related stress has increased. A large proportion of workers from all demographics claim to be dissatisfied with the way their work is structured and the way they are managed.
sentences.zh: 与工作相关的压力也增加了。来自各类人群的很大一部分员工声称，对自己工作的组织方式以及所受的管理方式感到不满。

grammar.type: claim to do + 并列宾语
grammar.note: 首句 Work-related stress has increased；后句主干 A large proportion of workers... claim to be dissatisfied with...，the way their work is structured 与 the way they are managed 为并列宾语，均含定语从句（省略 in which）。

sentences.en: This begs the question: what has gone wrong?
sentences.zh: 这就引出了一个问题：到底哪里出了错？

grammar.type: 冒号 + 特殊疑问句
grammar.note: 主句 This begs the question，beg the question 此处意为“引出问题”；冒号后 what has gone wrong 为具体问题（现在完成时）。

words.def: 引出问题；使人不禁要问

sentences.en: Why is it that on paper the drive for organisation seems a sure shot for increasing productivity, but in reality falls well short of what is expected?
sentences.zh: 为什么在纸面上，追求组织化似乎是提高生产率的万无一失之策，但在现实中却远远达不到预期呢？

grammar.type: 强调句疑问 + but 对比 + 宾语从句
grammar.note: Why is it that...? 为强调结构的疑问；从句 on paper the drive for organisation seems a sure shot..., but in reality falls well short of what is expected，but 连接对比，what is expected 为介词宾语从句；a sure shot 意为“十拿九稳之事”，fall short of 意为“达不到”。

sentences.en: This has been a problem for a while now. Frederick Taylor was one of the forefathers of scientific management.
sentences.zh: 这个问题已经存在有一段时间了。弗雷德里克·泰勒是科学管理的先驱之一。

grammar.type: 现在完成时 + 主系表
grammar.note: 首句 This has been a problem for a while now，for a while 表持续；后句 Frederick Taylor was one of the forefathers of scientific management，forefather 意为“先驱、鼻祖”。

sentences.en: Writing in the first half of the 20th century, he designed a number of principles to improve the efficiency of the work process, which have since become widespread in modern companies.
sentences.zh: 泰勒在20世纪上半叶著书立说，设计了一系列旨在提高工作流程效率的原则，这些原则此后在现代企业中变得普遍。

grammar.type: 现在分词状语 + 不定式目的 + 非限制性定语从句
grammar.note: Writing in the first half of the 20th century 为现在分词状语；主句 he designed a number of principles；to improve the efficiency of the work process 为不定式目的状语；which have since become widespread 为非限制性定语从句修饰 principles。

sentences.en: So the approach has been around for a while.
sentences.zh: 所以这种方法已经存在有一段时间了。

grammar.type: 现在完成时
grammar.note: 主干 the approach has been around for a while，be around 意为“存在、出现”；So 表承接推论。

sentences.en: New research suggests that this obsession with efficiency is misguided.
sentences.zh: 新的研究表明，这种对效率的痴迷是被误导的。

grammar.type: 宾语从句
grammar.note: 主干 New research suggests that...；从句 this obsession with efficiency is misguided，obsession with 意为“对……的痴迷”，misguided 意为“被误导的、错误的”。

sentences.en: The problem is not necessarily the management theories or strategies we use to organise our work; it’s the basic assumptions we hold in approaching how we work.
sentences.zh: 问题未必出在我们用来组织工作的管理理论或策略上；而在于我们在看待自己如何工作时所持的基本假设。

grammar.type: 分号并列 + 省略关系词定语从句
grammar.note: 分号连接两分句；前句 The problem is not necessarily the management theories or strategies (that) we use to organise our work，含省略关系词定语从句；后句 it's the basic assumptions (that) we hold in approaching how we work，同样含省略关系词定语从句，how we work 为 approaching 的宾语从句。

sentences.en: Here it’s the assumption that order is a necessary condition for productivity.
sentences.zh: 这里所说的，是“秩序是生产率的必要条件”这一假设。

grammar.type: 同位语从句
grammar.note: 主干 it's the assumption；that order is a necessary condition for productivity 为同位语从句，说明 assumption 的具体内容。

sentences.en: This assumption has also fostered the idea that disorder must be detrimental to organisational productivity.
sentences.zh: 这一假设还催生了一种观念：无序必然有损于组织的生产率。

grammar.type: 同位语从句
grammar.note: 主干 This assumption has also fostered the idea；that disorder must be detrimental to organisational productivity 为同位语从句说明 idea，be detrimental to 意为“对……有害”。

sentences.en: The result is that businesses and people spend time and money organising themselves for the sake of organising, rather than actually looking at the end goal and usefulness of such an effort.
sentences.zh: 其结果是，企业和个人花费时间与金钱进行组织，只是为了组织而组织，而不是真正去审视这种努力的最终目标和实际用处。

grammar.type: 表语从句 + rather than 对比
grammar.note: 主干 The result is that...；从句 businesses and people spend time and money organising themselves for the sake of organising；rather than actually looking at the end goal and usefulness 用 rather than + 动名词构成对比，for the sake of 意为“为了……的缘故”。

sentences.en: What’s more, recent studies show that order actually has diminishing returns.
sentences.zh: 更重要的是，近期的研究表明，秩序实际上存在着递减的回报。

grammar.type: 宾语从句 + 插入语
grammar.note: What's more 为插入语，意为“而且、更重要的是”；主干 recent studies show that...；从句 order actually has diminishing returns，diminishing returns 为经济学术语“报酬递减”。

sentences.en: Order does increase productivity to a certain extent, but eventually the usefulness of the process of organisation, and the benefit it yields, reduce until the point where any further increase in order reduces productivity.
sentences.zh: 秩序在一定程度上确实能提高生产率，但组织这一过程的有用性及其带来的收益最终会递减，直到某一临界点——在那里，秩序的任何进一步增加反而会降低生产率。

grammar.type: but 转折 + 省略关系词定语从句 + until/where 从句
grammar.note: 前句 Order does increase productivity to a certain extent，does 强调；but 后主语 the usefulness... and the benefit (that) it yields（含省略关系词定语从句），谓语 reduce；until the point where any further increase in order reduces productivity，where 引导定语从句修饰 point。

sentences.en: Some argue that in a business, if the cost of formally structuring something outweighs the benefit of doing it, then that thing ought not to be formally structured.
sentences.zh: 一些人认为，在企业中，如果正式地组织某件事的成本超过了这样做的收益，那么这件事就不应当被正式地组织。

grammar.type: 宾语从句 + if 条件从句
grammar.note: 主干 Some argue that...；从句内含 if the cost of formally structuring something outweighs the benefit of doing it 条件从句，主句 that thing ought not to be formally structured，outweigh 意为“重于、超过”。

sentences.en: Instead, the resources involved can be better used elsewhere.
sentences.zh: 相反，所涉及的资源可以更好地用在别处。

grammar.type: 过去分词定语 + 被动
grammar.note: 主干 the resources... can be better used elsewhere（被动）；involved 为过去分词作 resources 的后置定语；Instead 表转折。

sentences.en: In fact, research shows that, when innovating, the best approach is to create an environment devoid of structure and hierarchy and enable everyone involved to engage as one organic group.
sentences.zh: 事实上，研究表明，在进行创新时，最佳的做法是营造一个没有固定结构和层级的环境，让所有参与者作为一个有机整体协同投入。

grammar.type: 宾语从句 + 省略式时间状语 + 表语并列
grammar.note: 主干 research shows that...；when innovating 为省略主语的时间状语从句；从句主干 the best approach is to create an environment... and enable everyone... to engage...，两个不定式 to create 与 to enable 并列作表语；devoid of structure and hierarchy 为形容词短语定语，involved 为过去分词定语修饰 everyone。

sentences.en: These environments can lead to new solutions that, under conventionally structured environments (filled with bottlenecks in terms of information flow, power structures, rules, and routines) would never be reached.
sentences.zh: 这样的环境能够催生新的解决方案，而这些方案在传统结构化的环境（在信息流通、权力结构、规则和惯例方面充满瓶颈）下是永远无法达成的。

grammar.type: 定语从句 + 状语插入 + 括号补充
grammar.note: 主干 These environments can lead to new solutions；that... would never be reached 为定语从句修饰 solutions；under conventionally structured environments 为条件状语插入从句中，括号内 filled with bottlenecks... 为对该环境的补充说明。

sentences.en: In recent times companies have slowly started to embrace this disorganisation.
sentences.zh: 近来，公司已经开始慢慢接纳这种无组织化。

grammar.type: 现在完成时 + start to do
grammar.note: 主干 companies have slowly started to embrace this disorganisation；embrace 意为“欣然接受、拥抱”；In recent times 为时间状语。

sentences.en: Many of them embrace it in terms of perception (embracing the idea of disorder, as opposed to fearing it) and in terms of process (putting mechanisms in place to reduce structure).
sentences.zh: 它们中的许多既在观念层面接纳它（欣然接受无序的理念，而非畏惧它），也在流程层面接纳它（建立相应机制以减少结构化）。

grammar.type: in terms of 并列 + 括号解释
grammar.note: 主干 Many of them embrace it in terms of perception... and in terms of process...，两个 in terms of 短语并列；两处括号分别解释：as opposed to fearing it 意为“与畏惧它相对”，putting mechanisms in place 意为“建立机制”。

sentences.en: For example, Oticon, a large Danish manufacturer of hearing aids, used what it called a ‘spaghetti’ structure in order to reduce the organisation’s rigid hierarchies.
sentences.zh: 例如，丹麦大型助听器制造商奥迪康采用了它所称的“意大利面条式”结构，以削减该组织僵化的层级。

grammar.type: 同位语 + 宾语从句 + 不定式目的
grammar.note: a large Danish manufacturer of hearing aids 为 Oticon 的同位语；主干 Oticon used what it called a 'spaghetti' structure，what it called... 为宾语从句；in order to reduce the organisation's rigid hierarchies 为目的状语。

sentences.en: This involved scrapping formal job titles and giving staff huge amounts of ownership over their own time and projects.
sentences.zh: 这包括取消正式的职务头衔，并让员工对自己的时间和项目拥有极大的自主支配权。

grammar.type: 动名词并列宾语
grammar.note: 主干 This involved... ，involve 后接两个并列动名词短语 scrapping formal job titles 与 giving staff huge amounts of ownership...；over their own time and projects 为 ownership 的后置定语。

sentences.en: This approach proved to be highly successful initially, with clear improvements in worker productivity in all facets of the business.
sentences.zh: 这种做法起初被证明非常成功，企业各个方面的员工生产率都有明显提高。

grammar.type: prove to be + with 复合结构
grammar.note: 主干 This approach proved to be highly successful initially；with clear improvements in worker productivity in all facets of the business 为 with 引导的独立结构作伴随状语；facet 意为“方面”。

sentences.en: In similar fashion, the former chairman of General Electric embraced disorganisation, putting forward the idea of the ‘boundaryless’ organisation.
sentences.zh: 以类似的方式，通用电气的前任董事长也接纳了无组织化，提出了“无边界”组织的理念。

grammar.type: 现在分词状语
grammar.note: 主干 the former chairman of General Electric embraced disorganisation；putting forward the idea of the 'boundaryless' organisation 为现在分词状语；In similar fashion 意为“以类似方式”。

sentences.en: Again, it involves breaking down the barriers between different parts of a company and encouraging virtual collaboration and flexible working.
sentences.zh: 同样，它意味着打破公司不同部门之间的壁垒，鼓励虚拟协作和灵活办公。

grammar.type: 动名词并列宾语
grammar.note: 主干 it involves...，involve 后接两个并列动名词短语 breaking down the barriers... 与 encouraging virtual collaboration and flexible working。

sentences.en: Google and a number of other tech companies have embraced (at least in part) these kinds of flexible structures, facilitated by technology and strong company values which glue people together.
sentences.zh: 谷歌以及其他一些科技公司（至少在一定程度上）接纳了这类灵活的结构，这得益于技术以及将员工凝聚在一起的强大企业价值观。

grammar.type: 现在完成时 + 过去分词状语 + 定语从句
grammar.note: 主干 Google and a number of other tech companies have embraced... these kinds of flexible structures；facilitated by technology and strong company values 为过去分词状语；which glue people together 为定语从句修饰 values，glue... together 意为“把……粘合在一起”。

sentences.en: A word of warning to others thinking of jumping on this bandwagon: the evidence so far suggests disorder, much like order, also seems to have diminishing utility, and can also have detrimental effects on performance if overused.
sentences.zh: 对那些想要跟风效仿的人提个醒：迄今为止的证据表明，无序和秩序一样，其效用也会递减，如果被过度使用，同样会对绩效产生不利影响。

grammar.type: 冒号解释 + 插入类比 + if 从句
grammar.note: 冒号前 A word of warning to others thinking of jumping on this bandwagon 为提示语，thinking of... 为现在分词定语，jump on the bandwagon 意为“赶潮流、随大流”；冒号后 the evidence... suggests (that) disorder... also seems to have diminishing utility, and can also have detrimental effects...，much like order 为插入类比，if overused 为省略式条件状语从句。

sentences.en: Like order, disorder should be embraced only so far as it is useful. But we should not fear it – nor venerate one over the other.
sentences.zh: 和秩序一样，无序也只应在其有用的限度内被接纳。但我们不应畏惧它——也不应把其中一方奉为高于另一方。

grammar.type: so far as 状语 + nor 倒装
grammar.note: 首句 disorder should be embraced only so far as it is useful，so far as 意为“到……的程度、只要”；后句 we should not fear it，破折号后 nor venerate one over the other，nor 引导否定并列（此处省略了倒装主语，承接前句否定）；venerate A over B 意为“尊崇A胜过B”。

sentences.en: This research also shows that we should continually question whether or not our existing assumptions work.
sentences.zh: 这项研究还表明，我们应当不断质疑现有的假设是否行得通。

grammar.type: 宾语从句 + whether 从句
grammar.note: 主干 This research also shows that...；从句 we should continually question whether or not our existing assumptions work，whether or not... 为 question 的宾语从句。

questions.title: Questions 27–34
instructions: Reading Passage 3 has eight sections, A–H.
Choose the correct heading for each section from the list of headings below.
Write the correct number, i–ix, in boxes 27–34 on your answer sheet.
i Complaints about the impact of a certain approach
ii Fundamental beliefs that are in fact incorrect
iii Early recommendations concerning business activities
iv Organisations that put a new approach into practice
v Companies that have suffered from changing their approach
vi What people are increasingly expected to do
vii How to achieve outcomes that are currently impossible
viii Neither approach guarantees continuous improvement
ix Evidence that a certain approach can have more disadvantages than advantages

questions.title: Questions 35–37
instructions: Complete the sentences below.
Choose ONE WORD ONLY from the passage for each answer.
Write your answers in boxes 35–37 on your answer sheet.

items.prompt: Numerous training sessions are aimed at people who feel they are not ____ enough.
Being organised appeals to people who regard themselves as ____ .
Many people feel ____ with aspects of their work.

questions.title: Questions 38–40
instructions: Do the following statements agree with the information given in Reading Passage 3?
In boxes 38–40 on your answer sheet, write
TRUE if the statement agrees with the information
FALSE if the statement contradicts the information
NOT GIVEN if there is no information on this

items.prompt: Both businesses and people aim at order without really considering its value.
Innovation is most successful if the people involved have distinct roles.
Google was inspired to adopt flexibility by the success of General Electric.
